domains.name: Consent Management
domains.description: Collection, validity, and withdrawal of consent.

domains.name: Data Subject Rights
domains.description: Fulfillment of access, correction, and portability requests.

domains.name: Data Lifecycle
domains.description: Retention and secure disposal of personal data.

domains.name: Breach Response
domains.description: Mandatory data breach detection and notification.

domains.name: Cross Border Transfer
domains.description: Conditions for data transmission outside Malaysia.

domains.name: Governance Accountability
domains.description: Organizational data protection oversight and DPO functions.

domains.name: Registration Compliance
domains.description: Class of Data Users registration and fees.

controls.name: Consent Collection Mechanism
controls.description: Implement systematic consent collection across all data collection channels ensuring compliance with s6 and s7 requirements. Consent must be freely given, specific, informed, and unambiguous per the PDPA s2 definition, with separate consent obtained for each distinct processing purpose
keyActivities: Design consent forms and flows incorporating all s7 notice elements
Implement granular, purpose-specific consent checkboxes with no pre-ticking
Ensure consent is unbundled from acceptance of terms and conditions
Capture and store timestamped consent records with notice version presented
Validate consent mechanisms against PDPA s2 definition before deployment

maturity.basic: Paper-based consent forms collected at point of data collection; manual filing of signed forms; no systematic linkage between consent records and data subject profiles
maturity.mature: Digital consent forms with purpose-specific consent fields; automated timestamp and version recording; consent records stored in CRM or consent management system; annual consent form review against current notice
maturity.advanced: Centralised consent management platform integrated across all collection channels (web, app, physical, call centre) with real-time consent status propagation, automated preference propagation to downstream systems, consent analytics dashboard, and automated consent expiry management

healthcare.additionalRequirements: Granular patient consent: separate for treatment, billing, insurance, research; emergency vital interests exception documented

controls.name: Consent Record-Keeping and Evidence Management
controls.description: Maintain auditable records of all consents obtained, including the content of the notice presented at the time of consent, the specific options selected by the data subject, the collection timestamp and channel, and the complete consent lifecycle including any subsequent withdrawal
keyActivities: Store consent records linked to data subject identity with tamper-evident audit trail
Retain the exact version of the notice or consent form presented at time of consent
Record consent channel, IP address (for digital), and any agent-assisted collection details
Maintain complete consent lifecycle: grant, modification, and withdrawal history
Implement retention controls for consent records proportionate to processing period plus limitation periods

maturity.basic: Physical consent form copies filed in HR or customer files; spreadsheet log of consent dates; limited searchability
maturity.mature: Digital consent records in CRM or dedicated system; searchable by data subject; linked to processing activities; consent record accessible for DSAR responses
maturity.advanced: Immutable consent audit log with cryptographic timestamping; automated consent evidence retrieval for DSAR and regulatory requests; integration with ROPA showing consent coverage per processing activity; automated alerts for consent approaching expiry

controls.name: Consent Withdrawal Management
controls.description: Implement mechanisms for data subjects to withdraw consent as easily as it was given under PDPA s38, and ensure that upon withdrawal all downstream processing ceases and processors and third-party recipients are notified where required
keyActivities: Provide withdrawal mechanisms on every channel used for consent collection
Confirm withdrawal to data subject within a reasonable period (within 14 days)
Update consent register and propagate withdrawal status to all processing systems
Notify data processors and downstream recipients of withdrawal where personal data has been shared
Assess whether any alternative lawful basis exists before ceasing processing; document outcome

maturity.basic: Withdrawal accepted via written request to DPO; manual update of consent records; manual notification to processors via email
maturity.mature: Online withdrawal form or account preference centre; automated consent register update; workflow-based notification to processors; completion tracking
maturity.advanced: Real-time withdrawal propagation across all systems via API integration; automated processor notification; confirmation to data subject on completion; withdrawal analytics to identify systemic withdrawal causes

toolExamples: OneTrust Universal Consent and Preference Management

controls.name: Sensitive Personal Data Explicit Consent Control
controls.description: Apply heightened consent controls for the collection and processing of sensitive personal data under PDPA s29 (health data, political opinions, religious beliefs, criminal offences, biometric data), requiring explicit and specific consent beyond the standard consent requirement
keyActivities: Identify all processing activities involving sensitive personal data under s29 categories
Design explicit consent mechanisms for sensitive data — standalone, clearly highlighted, cannot be inferred from general consent
Train staff collecting sensitive data on the heightened consent standard
Implement technical controls blocking sensitive data collection without affirmative explicit consent
Implement enhanced access controls for stored sensitive personal data

maturity.basic: Separate consent form section for sensitive data; staff instructed not to collect without explicit consent; paper records retained
maturity.mature: Separate consent fields flagged in system with sensitive data indicator; access to sensitive data fields restricted to authorised roles; consent records for sensitive data retained separately
maturity.advanced: Automated sensitive data detection (DLP) preventing capture without consent token; encrypted storage of sensitive data with separate access controls; real-time monitoring of sensitive data access; automated DPIA trigger for sensitive data processing activities

toolExamples: Consent management with data classification integration
Vault or key management for sensitive data encryption

healthcare.additionalRequirements: All patient data is sensitive by definition; research consent specific per project; ethics committee approval prerequisite

insurance.additionalRequirements: Underwriting health data requires enhanced protections; genetic data access limited to named officers; biometric auth data not cross-used for underwriting

aviation.additionalRequirements: Biometric data requires explicit consent except government-mandated immigration; DPIA before biometric system deployment

communications.additionalRequirements: SIM registration biometric data treated as high-risk; location data consent specific per purpose

controls.name: Consent Validity Assessment and Remediation
controls.description: Periodically assess existing consent records against current PDPA requirements to identify deficient or invalid consents (e.g., pre-ticked boxes, bundled consents, consents obtained under legacy practices) and implement remediation including re-consent campaigns where necessary
keyActivities: Conduct annual audit of consent mechanisms against current PDPA requirements and guidance
Identify consent records that do not meet current validity standards
Design and execute re-consent campaigns for affected data subjects
Cease processing for data subjects who do not re-consent and where no alternative lawful basis applies
Update consent design based on assessment findings to prevent recurrence

maturity.basic: Ad hoc review of consent forms when PDPA guidance is updated; re-consent by postal mail where required
maturity.mature: Annual consent validity review against regulatory guidance; targeted re-consent campaigns by email with tracking of responses; documentation of assessment findings
maturity.advanced: Automated consent validity scoring against configurable rules; continuous monitoring of regulatory guidance changes; automated re-consent workflow with fallback to consent withdrawal if no response received; board reporting on consent validity metrics

controls.name: Data Subject Rights Request Intake and Identity Verification
controls.description: Operate a systematic process for receiving data subject rights requests through accessible channels, authenticating the identity of requesters before processing, and logging all requests with statutory deadline tracking in compliance with PDPA s30 and related sections
keyActivities: Provide accessible request channels (web form, email, post, in-person) clearly communicated in privacy notice
Acknowledge all requests within 3 business days with unique reference number
Apply proportionate identity verification commensurate with sensitivity of data held
Log request in DSAR register with receipt date, type, and 21-day statutory deadline
Assign request to appropriate handler and notify DPO

maturity.basic: DSARs accepted by email to DPO; manual tracking on spreadsheet; identity verified via email confirmation; deadline tracked manually
maturity.mature: Dedicated DSAR web portal with structured form; automated acknowledgement and reference generation; identity verification via government ID upload; DSAR case management system with deadline alerts
maturity.advanced: Omnichannel DSAR intake with automated routing; AI-assisted identity proofing with liveness detection for digital channel; real-time DSAR dashboard for DPO; automated statutory deadline escalation to management

controls.name: Data Subject Access Request Fulfilment
controls.description: Systematically search, compile, review, and provide data subjects with access to their personal data held by the organization within the 21-day statutory period under PDPA s30, applying appropriate exemptions and redactions where permissible
keyActivities: Execute a comprehensive search across all identified personal data systems against the ROPA
Compile search results and remove third-party personal data and information subject to legal privilege
Review for applicable statutory exemptions (national security, crime detection, legal proceedings)
Prepare and transmit access response in clear, plain language within 21 days
Document search scope, exemptions applied, and rationale for any redactions

maturity.basic: Manual search of known systems by DPO; Excel compilation of results; PDF package sent by email; exemptions checked against Act provisions manually
maturity.mature: Structured search playbook covering all ROPA systems; redaction tool for third-party data; response template library; fee assessment and waiver procedure; documented exemption analysis
maturity.advanced: Automated personal data discovery and extraction from integrated systems; automated third-party data redaction; secure data subject portal for response delivery; complete audit trail of search and compilation steps; metrics on response quality and timeliness

toolExamples: OneTrust DSAR with data discovery integration

controls.name: Data Correction Request Handling
controls.description: Process and fulfil data subject correction requests under PDPA s34 within statutory timelines, amending inaccurate or incomplete personal data across relevant systems, notifying downstream recipients, and communicating outcomes to the data subject
keyActivities: Accept and log correction requests with clear evidentiary requirements communicated to the data subject
Assess accuracy of existing data against evidence provided by data subject
Implement corrections across all systems holding the relevant data, not just the presenting system
Notify data processors and third-party recipients of data corrections within a reasonable period
Communicate outcome to data subject with explanation where correction is declined

maturity.basic: Manual correction request form; DPO coordinates with system owners for correction; notification to third parties by email; response by letter
maturity.mature: Correction request tracked in DSAR system; documented accuracy assessment; systematic notification list for downstream recipients; standard response templates; correction log maintained
maturity.advanced: Automated correction propagation to integrated systems via API; master data management integration for single source of truth; automated notification to processors and recipients; data subject portal showing correction status and completion

toolExamples: Master Data Management platforms (Informatica, SAP MDM)

controls.name: Direct Marketing Opt-Out Management
controls.description: Operate a systematic mechanism for data subjects to opt out of direct marketing communications under PDPA s43, ensuring opt-outs are processed promptly, suppression lists are maintained and applied across all marketing channels, and do-not-contact preferences are respected
keyActivities: Include a clear, easy-to-use opt-out mechanism in every direct marketing communication
Process opt-out requests within 10 business days and suppress all marketing to opted-out contacts
Maintain a suppression list and apply it before each marketing send across all channels
Cross-reference suppression list with acquired third-party marketing lists before use
Monitor re-opt-in rates and review marketing consent practices periodically

maturity.basic: Unsubscribe link in emails; manual suppression list; DNC requests handled by marketing team; not consistently applied across all channels
maturity.mature: Automated unsubscribe processing via ESP; central suppression list applied in CRM; marketing platform checks against suppression before each campaign; DNC list checked against MCMC registry
maturity.advanced: Universal preference centre managing opt-outs across all channels (email, SMS, post, calls); real-time suppression propagation to all marketing systems; automated audit of suppression list application; analytics on opt-out rates by channel and campaign

toolExamples: Mailchimp / Salesforce Marketing Cloud unsubscribe management
MCMC Do Not Call Registry integration

controls.name: DSAR Deadline and Escalation Management
controls.description: Monitor and enforce statutory response deadlines for all data subject rights requests, with automated alerts and escalation procedures to ensure the 21-day response period under PDPA s30 and s34 is met consistently, and that extensions are justified and communicated to data subjects
keyActivities: Automatically calculate and display statutory deadline (21 calendar days from verified receipt) for each request
Implement tiered alert system at day 10, day 16, and day 20 to responsible handler and DPO
Document and communicate valid extension grounds to data subject before deadline expires
Track extension timelines and second-level deadlines for extended requests
Report on DSAR response performance (on-time rate, average response time) to management quarterly

maturity.basic: Manual calendar entries for DSAR deadlines; DPO manually monitors open requests; extensions communicated ad hoc
maturity.mature: DSAR case management system with built-in deadline tracking; automated email alerts to handler and DPO; extension logged with reason code; monthly DSAR performance report
maturity.advanced: Real-time DSAR dashboard with SLA traffic-light status; automated escalation to senior management for at-risk requests; extension workflow requiring DPO approval; quarterly performance reporting to board; benchmarking against industry response times

toolExamples: Jira Service Desk with SLA plugin

controls.name: Data Subject Rights Awareness Communication
controls.description: Ensure data subjects are clearly informed of their rights under PDPA (access, correction, withdrawal of consent, opt-out from direct marketing) in all privacy notices and at relevant touchpoints, in plain language accessible to the intended audience including in Bahasa Malaysia
keyActivities: Include clear description of all data subject rights in all privacy notices and consent forms
Provide practical instructions for how to exercise each right (contact details, form link, process)
Ensure rights information is available in Bahasa Malaysia as required under s7
Display DPO contact information prominently in all notices and on the organization's website
Conduct periodic review of rights communication clarity and accessibility

maturity.basic: Rights stated in privacy notice in legal language; DPO email address provided; notice available in BM and EN on website
maturity.mature: Plain-language explanation of rights in all notices; dedicated 'Your Rights' webpage; how-to guide for exercising each right; DPO contact on every communication
maturity.advanced: Interactive rights exercise portal with step-by-step guidance; multilingual support beyond BM/EN; chatbot for rights guidance; accessibility-compliant format (WCAG); data subject feedback mechanism on rights exercise experience

controls.name: Privacy Notice Design and Content Compliance
controls.description: Design and maintain privacy notices that contain all elements required by PDPA s7 (Notice and Choice Principle), presented in a clear and intelligible format in both Bahasa Malaysia and English, and updated whenever processing activities materially change
keyActivities: Audit all data collection touchpoints to identify where s7 notices must be provided
Ensure every notice includes all mandatory elements: controller identity, purposes, consent rights, disclosure categories, transfer information, and data subject rights
Draft notices in plain language at an accessible reading level
Maintain bilingual (BM and EN) versions and verify translation accuracy
Review and update notices whenever data processing activities change materially

maturity.basic: Single generic privacy notice covering all processing; annual review by legal; BM translation completed at notice creation but not systematically updated
maturity.mature: Purpose-specific or audience-specific notices (employee, customer, website); layered notice approach with short-form and long-form; annual compliance review against s7 checklist; BM and EN versions maintained in sync
maturity.advanced: Dynamic privacy notice generated from ROPA data; automated gap analysis against s7 checklist; version-controlled notice library; notice testing with data subject panels; notice effectiveness metrics (read rates, comprehension scores)

controls.name: Privacy Notice Delivery and Accessibility
controls.description: Ensure data protection notices are delivered to data subjects at or before the time of personal data collection through appropriate channels, and remain accessible throughout the processing relationship, in compliance with PDPA s7
keyActivities: Map every data collection touchpoint to the applicable notice and verify notice is presented before or at collection
Ensure notice is accessible year-round on the organization's website and physical premises
For digital channels, implement technical checks that notice is presented before data submission
For physical collection, maintain printed notice copies at all collection points
Implement notice change communication process to notify data subjects of material changes

maturity.basic: Notice linked in website footer; physical copy available on request; new employees given notice at onboarding
maturity.mature: Notice displayed at every digital data collection form; mandatory notice acknowledgement for new customer onboarding; email notification to existing data subjects of material notice changes
maturity.advanced: Just-in-time contextual notices at each data element collection point; notice interaction tracking (opened, clicked, time on page); automated re-notification workflow on material change; A/B testing of notice presentation formats for comprehension

controls.name: Privacy Notice Review and Update Cycle
controls.description: Maintain a regular review cycle for all privacy notices and data protection disclosures to ensure they remain accurate, complete, and aligned with current processing activities, regulatory guidance, and organizational changes
keyActivities: Schedule annual review of all notices with DPO sign-off as minimum
Trigger ad hoc notice review upon: new processing activity, new disclosure category, system change, regulatory guidance update, or organizational restructure
Compare current notice content against ROPA to identify gaps or inaccuracies
Maintain version history and effective dates for all notices
Document review outcomes and material changes requiring data subject notification

maturity.basic: Annual notice review by DPO and legal; version updated and published; old version archived
maturity.mature: Change trigger register linking ROPA changes to notice review obligations; structured review against s7 checklist; management sign-off for material changes; version control with diff between versions
maturity.advanced: Automated ROPA-to-notice gap analysis; continuous monitoring of regulatory guidance and Commissioner issuances for notice implications; notice change impact assessment; automated data subject notification workflow for material changes

controls.name: CCTV and Monitoring Disclosure
controls.description: Ensure transparent disclosure of workplace and premises surveillance (CCTV, access monitoring, email monitoring, location tracking) through physical notices at monitoring locations and disclosure in applicable privacy notices, in compliance with PDPA s7
keyActivities: Map all monitoring and surveillance technologies in operation
Install CCTV notices compliant with Commissioner guidance at camera locations and premises entrances
Include monitoring disclosure in employee data protection notice and acceptable use policy
Disclose CCTV monitoring purposes, retention period, and access procedures
Review monitoring disclosure adequacy annually and when monitoring scope changes

maturity.basic: CCTV signage at premises entrance; employee notice covers CCTV monitoring; footage retained 30 days
maturity.mature: CCTV notice at each camera zone and building entrance meeting s7 requirements; employee monitoring section in acceptable use policy; access logs for CCTV footage reviewed quarterly
maturity.advanced: Comprehensive monitoring inventory including all digital monitoring (email, keyloggers, location tracking); layered notice architecture with short-form signage and full notice at reception; annual employee survey on monitoring awareness; DPIA for high-risk monitoring technologies

toolExamples: Physical notice templates compliant with Commissioner guidance
CCTV management software with audit logs

controls.name: Information Security Risk Management
controls.description: Implement a structured information security risk management process to identify, assess, treat, and monitor risks to personal data, ensuring that security measures are proportionate to the risk of harm as required under PDPA s9 and the Personal Data Protection Standards 2015
keyActivities: Conduct annual information security risk assessment scoped to personal data processing activities
Maintain a risk register with risk ratings, treatment plans, and risk owners
Select and implement security controls proportionate to assessed risk levels
Review risk register quarterly and update following incidents or material changes
Report top security risks to senior management and board annually

maturity.basic: Annual IT security review by IT manager; ad hoc risk identification; security controls implemented based on best efforts; no formal risk register
maturity.mature: Formal risk assessment methodology (ISO 27001, NIST); risk register maintained in GRC tool; risk treatment plans tracked to completion; management risk reporting; annual penetration testing
maturity.advanced: Continuous risk monitoring with automated vulnerability scanning and threat intelligence integration; risk-based security investment decisions; integration with enterprise risk management; real-time risk posture dashboard; third-party security risk management integrated into vendor onboarding

controls.name: Access Control and Identity Management
controls.description: Implement role-based access controls and identity management for all systems processing personal data, applying least-privilege and need-to-know principles to minimize the risk of unauthorized access, in compliance with PDPA s9 Security Principle and s12 Access Principle
keyActivities: Assign system access based on job role and minimum necessary data access (least privilege)
Implement multi-factor authentication for all systems processing personal data
Conduct quarterly access reviews and revoke unnecessary permissions
Immediately de-provision access upon employee departure or role change
Maintain access logs for all personal data systems and review anomalies monthly

maturity.basic: Username and password for all systems; ad hoc access reviews when role changes; IT-managed user accounts; shared accounts in some legacy systems
maturity.mature: RBAC implemented across major personal data systems; MFA for remote access and sensitive systems; quarterly access certification reviews; automated de-provisioning integrated with HR system; access logs reviewed by IT security monthly
maturity.advanced: Zero-trust network architecture; conditional access policies based on device compliance and risk score; privileged access management (PAM) for admin accounts with session recording; just-in-time access for sensitive data; continuous access review with automated anomaly detection; UEBA for insider threat detection

banking.additionalRequirements: MFA mandatory for all online/mobile banking access per BNM RMiT; device binding for mobile apps

healthcare.additionalRequirements: 2FA for EHR access; auto-logout after 10 minutes; emergency break-glass access with post-access review

communications.additionalRequirements: Privileged access management with MFA and additional logging for network admin accounts

controls.name: Encryption and Data Protection In-Transit and At-Rest
controls.description: Implement encryption controls to protect personal data during transmission and at rest, reducing the risk of unauthorized disclosure in the event of interception, theft, or unauthorized access, as required by PDPA s9 and the Personal Data Protection Standards 2015
keyActivities: Encrypt all personal data in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher for all web-facing services
Encrypt personal data at rest in databases, file systems, and backup media using AES-256 or equivalent
Implement full-disk encryption for all laptops, mobile devices, and portable media
Manage encryption keys with dedicated key management processes including key rotation
Prohibit transmission of personal data via unencrypted channels (plain email, FTP, HTTP)

maturity.basic: TLS on public-facing website; database-level encryption for primary databases; basic password protection on sensitive files; encryption policy in place
maturity.mature: TLS 1.2+ enforced across all internal and external services; AES-256 encryption at rest for all personal data stores; full-disk encryption on all endpoints; key management procedure documented; email encryption for sensitive communications
maturity.advanced: End-to-end encryption for high-sensitivity data flows; hardware security modules (HSMs) for key management; automated certificate management and renewal; data loss prevention integration to block unencrypted transmission; encryption health monitoring dashboard

healthcare.additionalRequirements: AES-256 mandatory for EHR; TLS 1.2+; 2FA; break-glass emergency access with automatic privacy officer alert

banking.additionalRequirements: MFA mandatory per BNM RMiT; PCI DSS for cardholder data; end-to-end encryption for online/mobile banking transactions

communications.additionalRequirements: End-to-end encryption for subscriber data; segregated network segments; SIEM for real-time monitoring

insurance.additionalRequirements: All health data encrypted at rest and in transit; role-based access for health/medical data

electricity.additionalRequirements: Encrypted smart meter communications; segregated AMI/MDMS networks

controls.name: Vulnerability and Patch Management
controls.description: Maintain a systematic process for identifying, assessing, and remediating vulnerabilities in systems that process personal data, ensuring software is kept patched and secure against known exploits that could lead to a personal data breach
keyActivities: Conduct automated vulnerability scanning on all personal data systems at least monthly
Triage and prioritise vulnerabilities based on CVSS score and exposure to personal data
Apply critical security patches within 72 hours; high-severity within 14 days; medium within 30 days
Track patch compliance rates and escalate non-compliance to IT management
Conduct annual penetration testing of internet-facing applications handling personal data

maturity.basic: Windows Update and software auto-updates enabled; annual vulnerability scan by IT; patch review at monthly IT meeting
maturity.mature: Dedicated vulnerability scanner (Qualys/Rapid7) scanning weekly; patch management platform (SCCM/Intune) with compliance reporting; risk-based patch prioritisation; monthly vulnerability report to IT management; annual penetration test
maturity.advanced: Continuous vulnerability scanning with real-time remediation workflows; integration with SIEM for active exploitation detection; attack surface management platform for internet-exposed assets; penetration testing twice yearly plus after major changes; vulnerability KPIs in board security report

banking.additionalRequirements: Annual penetration testing for internet-facing financial systems per BNM RMiT; PCI DSS quarterly ASV scans

communications.additionalRequirements: Penetration testing twice yearly minimum for subscriber data systems

healthcare.additionalRequirements: Annual penetration testing of EHR systems

controls.name: Security Monitoring and Incident Detection
controls.description: Implement security monitoring capabilities to detect unauthorized access, unusual data access patterns, and potential personal data breaches in real time, enabling rapid response to security events before they escalate into notifiable breaches under PDPA s12B
keyActivities: Collect and centralize security logs from all personal data systems into a SIEM platform
Define and tune detection rules for unauthorized access, bulk data export, and anomalous behaviour
Establish a 24/7 security monitoring capability or managed SOC with defined SLA for alert response
Create documented incident response playbooks for common breach scenarios
Review and update detection rules quarterly based on threat intelligence and incident history

maturity.basic: Server and firewall logs retained 90 days; IT administrator reviews logs on ad hoc basis; breach detected via user reports or system failure alerts
maturity.mature: SIEM deployed with correlation rules for key threat scenarios; security alerts reviewed by IT security daily; documented incident response procedure; breach notification checklist aligned with s12B
maturity.advanced: Managed SOC with 24/7 monitoring and 15-minute alert response SLA; UEBA for insider threat detection; threat intelligence integration; automated incident triage and severity classification; SOAR platform for automated containment actions; quarterly red team exercises

banking.additionalRequirements: Real-time fraud detection and anomaly monitoring for online banking; dual breach reporting to Commissioner and BNM

communications.additionalRequirements: SIEM capabilities for real-time subscriber data system access monitoring; threat intelligence sharing with MCMC

electricity.additionalRequirements: AMI network attack scenario coverage in IRP; comprehensive audit logging for consumption data access

controls.name: Physical Security Controls for Personal Data
controls.description: Implement physical security measures to protect personal data stored or processed in physical locations, including server rooms, offices, and physical record storage areas, preventing unauthorized physical access to systems and documents containing personal data
keyActivities: Restrict physical access to server rooms and data centres to authorised personnel only using access cards
Secure paper records containing personal data in locked storage with controlled access
Implement clear desk policy for workstations accessing personal data
Control visitor access to areas where personal data is processed
Maintain physical access logs for server rooms and sensitive areas

maturity.basic: Server room with locked door; key or swipe card access; paper records in locked filing cabinet; visitor sign-in book
maturity.mature: Card-access controlled server room with access log; CCTV in server room; clear desk policy enforced; secure destruction for paper personal data; visitor escort policy
maturity.advanced: Biometric access control for server room; CCTV with motion detection and 90-day retention; clean desk automated enforcement via physical security audit; hardware asset tagging and tracking; environmental monitoring (temperature, humidity) for server rooms

toolExamples: Secure document shredders and destruction services

aviation.additionalRequirements: CCTV notices at all camera locations; general footage max 30 days; incident footage subject to legal hold

controls.name: Data Loss Prevention (DLP)
controls.description: Deploy data loss prevention technology and controls to detect and prevent unauthorized exfiltration or disclosure of personal data through digital channels including email, cloud storage, removable media, and web uploads, reducing the risk of personal data breaches
keyActivities: Define DLP policies for personal data categories (name, NRIC, financial data, health data)
Deploy DLP at email gateway, endpoint, and web proxy to detect and block policy violations
Configure DLP to alert on bulk personal data transfers or exports from personal data systems
Implement removable media controls (USB block or encryption enforcement)
Review and tune DLP policy false positive and false negative rates monthly

maturity.basic: USB port restrictions via Group Policy; email warnings for large attachments; acceptable use policy prohibiting personal data exfiltration
maturity.mature: DLP deployed at email gateway with content inspection for NRIC, credit card numbers; USB restriction by policy with exception process; quarterly DLP policy review; DLP alerts reviewed by security team
maturity.advanced: Integrated DLP platform covering email, endpoint, web, and cloud; ML-based sensitive data classification integrated with DLP engine; context-aware DLP with risk-based blocking vs. alerting; DLP dashboards showing data flow risk; integration with SIEM for DLP events in security monitoring

controls.name: Data Minimisation at Collection
controls.description: Ensure that only personal data that is adequate, relevant, and necessary for the specified processing purpose is collected, aligned with the General Principle (s6) necessity requirement and the Data Integrity Principle (s11) principle of processing personal data appropriate to its purpose
keyActivities: Conduct data minimisation review for all data collection forms and systems before deployment
Require business justification for each data field collected linked to a specified processing purpose
Remove or make optional any data fields not strictly necessary for the stated purpose
Periodically review existing collection forms for data minimisation opportunities
Incorporate data minimisation review as a mandatory step in DPIA and privacy by design processes

maturity.basic: Ad hoc review of forms by IT or DPO; minimal guidance on data minimisation provided to form designers
maturity.mature: Data field justification register required for all new collection forms; DPO sign-off on new collection forms; annual audit of existing forms for minimisation opportunities
maturity.advanced: Automated data minimisation gate in SDLC pipeline; machine learning-assisted identification of redundant data fields in existing systems; data minimisation metrics (average fields per form vs. purpose) tracked in governance dashboard

toolExamples: Data cataloguing tools for field justification

controls.name: Retention Schedule Management and Enforcement
controls.description: Implement and enforce a comprehensive personal data retention schedule aligned with PDPA s10 (Retention Principle), ensuring personal data is not retained longer than necessary for the purpose for which it was collected, subject to legal hold requirements
keyActivities: Develop and maintain a data retention schedule covering all personal data categories and processing activities
Configure automated data deletion or anonymisation at retention period expiry in key personal data systems
Conduct annual review of retention schedule against regulatory and legal retention obligations
Implement legal hold process to suspend automatic deletion for data subject to litigation or investigation
Produce annual disposal report documenting data categories disposed of and disposal methods used

maturity.basic: Written retention schedule approved by DPO and legal; manual deletion of data by system owners when notified; annual reminder to review data holdings
maturity.mature: Retention schedule integrated with primary personal data systems for automated deletion prompts; legal hold tool integrated with retention system; quarterly disposal reporting; retention schedule annually reviewed against legal changes
maturity.advanced: Automated retention enforcement across all integrated systems via data governance platform; automated legal hold propagation from legal case management; real-time retention compliance dashboard; machine learning-assisted retention classification for unstructured data; integration with ROPA

banking.additionalRequirements: KYC/CDD records 6yr post-relationship (AMLA); transaction records 6yr; credit agreements 7yr; CCTV 90 days minimum

healthcare.additionalRequirements: Adult records 7yr post-treatment; minors until age 25 or 7yr; surgical 10yr; imaging 5yr; obstetric 25yr

communications.additionalRequirements: CDRs max 12 months; SIM registration subscription+2yr; granular location max 3 months without consent

insurance.additionalRequirements: Underwriting records policy term+7yr; claims settlement+7yr; telematics operational+2yr

electricity.additionalRequirements: Smart meter interval data max 13 months; consumer accounts 7yr post-relationship

water.additionalRequirements: Billing records 7yr post-relationship; field service max 5yr; field vehicle CCTV max 30 days

controls.name: Secure Data Disposal
controls.description: Implement secure disposal procedures for personal data that has reached end of retention period or is no longer needed, ensuring personal data cannot be recovered from disposed media or from decommissioned third-party systems, in compliance with PDPA s10 Retention Principle
keyActivities: Apply NIST 800-88 or equivalent data sanitisation standards for electronic media disposal
Maintain disposal certificates for all disposed media, hardware, and paper records
Use certified data destruction services for physical hardware and paper records
Verify deletion of personal data from cloud and SaaS platforms on contract termination
Track disposal activities in disposal log including date, method, data categories, and certification reference

maturity.basic: IT equipment wiped before disposal; paper records shredded; email to DPO when disposal complete; no formal certificate
maturity.mature: Documented disposal procedure per media type; certified third-party destruction service for hard drives; certificate of destruction obtained and filed; cloud/SaaS deletion verification on offboarding
maturity.advanced: Asset-level disposal tracking with automated retirement workflow; NIST 800-88 compliant sanitisation with verification; blockchain-based disposal certification; automated verification of processor data deletion via API; annual disposal effectiveness audit

healthcare.additionalRequirements: Disposal certificates mandatory; legacy paper-to-electronic conversion under appropriate security

banking.additionalRequirements: Financial records disposal must be documented with certificates of destruction

controls.name: Data Accuracy and Quality Management
controls.description: Implement controls to maintain the accuracy and completeness of personal data throughout its lifecycle, aligned with PDPA s11 Data Integrity Principle, which requires that personal data is accurate, complete, not misleading, and kept up to date
keyActivities: Implement data validation rules at point of collection to prevent inaccurate data entry
Establish periodic data quality reviews for critical personal data stores (customer, employee databases)
Enable and communicate data subject correction mechanism as a data quality input
Implement duplicate detection and merge processes to prevent conflicting records
Train staff on data accuracy obligations under s11 and the consequences of maintaining inaccurate data

maturity.basic: Basic form validation (required fields, format checks); manual data correction by staff on request; annual data quality review by IT
maturity.mature: Data validation rules in all data entry systems; regular data quality reports from CRM/ERP; customer self-service portal for data correction; duplicate detection in CRM; staff training on data accuracy
maturity.advanced: Master data management (MDM) platform as single source of truth; automated data quality scoring with alerts for degradation; continuous duplicate detection and merge; address and contact data validation via third-party APIs; data quality KPIs in operational dashboards

controls.name: Purpose Limitation Enforcement
controls.description: Implement controls ensuring personal data collected for a specified purpose is not processed in a manner incompatible with that purpose, aligned with the General Principle (s6) and Disclosure Principle (s8) requirements to process data only for the purposes stated at collection
keyActivities: Document permitted purposes for each data category in the ROPA and consent records
Implement technical access controls preventing use of personal data for non-authorised purposes
Conduct purpose compatibility assessment before any new use of existing personal data
Train staff that data collected for one purpose must not be used for another without further consent or lawful basis
Include purpose limitation requirements in data processing agreements with processors

maturity.basic: Staff instructed on purpose limitation in training; ROPA documents permitted purposes; ad hoc review of new data use cases
maturity.mature: Purpose limitation clause in all data processing agreements; formal purpose compatibility assessment required for new data uses; ROPA purpose fields reviewed when use cases change; access controls by purpose category in primary systems
maturity.advanced: Technical data labelling by purpose in data platform; automated enforcement preventing data flows outside permitted purpose boundaries; purpose limitation metrics tracked in governance dashboard; privacy by design gate for new data use cases requires DPO sign-off on purpose compatibility

toolExamples: Data catalogues with purpose tagging (Collibra, Alation)
API gateway policies for purpose-based access

controls.name: Data Breach Detection and Triage
controls.description: Implement controls for the timely detection of personal data breaches from multiple sources (security alerts, employee reports, third-party notifications, vulnerability scans) and a rapid triage process to assess notifiability under PDPA s12B within the 72-hour window
keyActivities: Establish and publicise multiple breach reporting channels (IT helpdesk, DPO email, security hotline)
Define and train staff on personal data breach indicators and reporting obligations
Implement automated security monitoring with breach-specific alert rules (see data-security domain)
Conduct initial triage within 4 hours of breach report: confirm, classify, and assess notifiability
Document triage outcomes including decision not to notify with rationale

maturity.basic: Staff instructed to report suspected breaches to IT and DPO; IT investigates and escalates; checklist for initial triage
maturity.mature: Dedicated breach reporting inbox with auto-acknowledgement; breach triage template with severity matrix; defined escalation path to DPO and CISO; 72-hour countdown starts on confirmation; breach register updated at triage
maturity.advanced: 24/7 breach detection through SOC integration; automated initial classification of security events as potential breaches; breach management platform tracking triage, notification, and remediation; regulatory countdown timer with automated escalation; API integration with Commissioner portal for notification

banking.additionalRequirements: Dual reporting to Commissioner under s12B and to BNM per incident reporting requirements

insurance.additionalRequirements: Dual reporting to Commissioner and BNM for significant security incidents

aviation.additionalRequirements: Notify both Commissioner and CAAM for significant passenger data breaches

controls.name: Mandatory Breach Notification to Commissioner
controls.description: Implement a process to ensure mandatory notification of notifiable personal data breaches to the Personal Data Protection Commissioner within 72 hours of becoming aware, as required by PDPA s12B inserted by Amendment Act A1727, with all prescribed notification content
keyActivities: Determine notifiability: assess whether breach is likely to result in harm to data subjects
Prepare notification using Commissioner's prescribed format including all mandatory content
Submit notification to Commissioner within 72 hours of becoming aware of the notifiable breach
Submit supplementary notification if full information is not available at initial notification
Retain copies of all notifications, submissions, and Commissioner correspondence

maturity.basic: DPO prepares notification manually from Commissioner guidance; submitted via email or portal within 72 hours; copy filed in DPO records
maturity.mature: Breach notification template pre-drafted against s12B requirements; notifiability assessment checklist; DPO and CISO jointly sign off on notification; legal review for significant breaches; timeline log documenting notification steps
maturity.advanced: Automated notification drafting from breach management platform data fields; real-time 72-hour countdown with alerts; legal and DPO review workflow integrated in platform; direct API submission to Commissioner portal where available; automated supplementary notification scheduling

toolExamples: Breach notification templates aligned with s12B

banking.additionalRequirements: Dual reporting to Commissioner under s12B and to BNM per incident reporting requirements

insurance.additionalRequirements: Dual reporting to Commissioner and BNM for significant security incidents

aviation.additionalRequirements: Notify both Commissioner and CAAM for significant passenger data breaches

controls.name: Breach Containment and Remediation
controls.description: Implement documented containment and remediation procedures for personal data breaches by breach type, ensuring the breach is stopped, the root cause is identified and addressed, and recurrence is prevented, as part of the overall breach response obligation under PDPA s12B and s9
keyActivities: Execute breach-type-specific containment playbooks (ransomware, unauthorized access, data loss, insider threat)
Preserve evidence for forensic investigation before containment actions where possible
Conduct root cause analysis to identify the technical, process, or human failure that enabled the breach
Develop and implement remediation action plan addressing identified root causes
Conduct post-incident review within 30 days and implement lessons learned

maturity.basic: IT team contains breach by isolating affected systems; DPO coordinates with management; remediation actions assigned in follow-up meeting; incident report filed
maturity.mature: Written incident response playbooks by breach type; forensic evidence preservation procedure; root cause analysis template; remediation action plan tracked to completion; post-incident review report shared with management
maturity.advanced: Cyber incident response retainer with specialist forensic firm; SOAR platform for automated containment actions (account lockout, network isolation); real-time threat intelligence integration during response; tabletop exercises twice yearly; breach remediation effectiveness metrics

controls.name: Data Subject Breach Notification
controls.description: Implement a process for notifying affected data subjects of a personal data breach where the breach is likely to result in harm to the data subjects, in compliance with PDPA s12B, including notification content, delivery methods, and timeline requirements
keyActivities: Assess harm likelihood and necessity of data subject notification based on breach nature and data type
Draft notification in plain language explaining the breach, data affected, potential harm, and protective steps
Select appropriate notification channel (email, letter, SMS, in-app, website notice) based on affected population
Deliver notification without undue delay once decision to notify is made
Establish dedicated helpline or contact point for affected data subjects

maturity.basic: DPO drafts notification letter; sent by email or post to affected data subjects; generic template adapted per incident
maturity.mature: Harm assessment framework for notification decisions; notification template library by channel; bulk email/SMS capability for large-scale breaches; dedicated helpline established for significant breaches; notification sent within 5 business days of notification decision
maturity.advanced: Dynamic notification platform with personalised content by affected data type; multi-channel delivery with delivery confirmation; credit monitoring or identity protection service offered for high-risk breaches; call centre script and FAQ prepared; regulatory-compliant notification content reviewed by legal and DPO

toolExamples: Email marketing platforms for bulk notification

controls.name: Cross-Border Transfer Identification and Mapping
controls.description: Identify and map all flows of personal data outside Malaysia including transfers to cloud services, SaaS platforms, offshore processors, group companies, and business partners, to ensure all cross-border transfers are subject to appropriate safeguards under PDPA s129
keyActivities: Conduct data flow mapping to identify all cross-border transfers (including cloud, SaaS, and email)
Classify transfers by recipient country, recipient type, and data categories transferred
Record all identified transfers in the cross-border transfer register
Review transfer register quarterly for new or changed transfers
Assess whether any identified transfers lack adequate transfer mechanisms and prioritise remediation

maturity.basic: Manual data flow mapping exercise conducted annually by DPO; spreadsheet of known international transfers; ad hoc review when new vendors are onboarded
maturity.mature: Data flow mapping integrated with vendor onboarding process; cross-border transfer register maintained in ROPA tool; quarterly review of transfer register; IT procurement required to declare international transfers at contract stage
maturity.advanced: Automated data flow discovery tool scanning network traffic for international data flows; integration with cloud access security broker (CASB) to detect unsanctioned cross-border SaaS usage; real-time transfer monitoring dashboard; automated alerts for new or unregistered international data flows

aviation.additionalRequirements: PNR/API transfers to foreign authorities require documentation under s129; adequacy or derogation basis required

healthcare.additionalRequirements: Overseas telehealth providers must have s129-compliant transfer mechanisms

controls.name: Transfer Mechanism Selection and Implementation
controls.description: Select and implement an appropriate transfer mechanism for each cross-border transfer of personal data under PDPA s129, including adequacy determination, standard contractual clauses, binding corporate rules, explicit consent, or certification schemes, and document the selection rationale
keyActivities: Determine whether the recipient country has an adequacy determination from the Malaysian Commissioner
Where no adequacy exists, select appropriate alternative mechanism (SCCs, BCRs, consent, certification)
Execute standard contractual clauses or other contractual safeguards before initiating transfer
Obtain DPO approval for each transfer mechanism selection and document rationale
Register transfer mechanisms with the Commissioner where required under s129

maturity.basic: DPO manually checks approved country list before authorising transfers; SCCs executed for major non-approved country transfers; limited documentation of mechanism selection rationale
maturity.mature: Transfer mechanism decision tree approved by DPO and legal; SCCs template library for different transfer scenarios; mechanism selection documented in transfer register; Commissioner notification completed for applicable transfers; annual review of mechanism adequacy
maturity.advanced: Automated adequacy assessment against current approved country list; SCC execution workflow integrated with vendor contracting; BCR program management for intra-group transfers; mechanism currency monitoring for regulatory changes (adequacy decisions, new SCCs); transfer mechanism compliance dashboard

toolExamples: OneTrust Vendor Risk for transfer mechanism management

aviation.additionalRequirements: PNR/API transfers to foreign authorities require documentation under s129; adequacy or derogation basis required

healthcare.additionalRequirements: Overseas telehealth providers must have s129-compliant transfer mechanisms

controls.name: Transfer Impact Assessment (TIA)
controls.description: Conduct Transfer Impact Assessments for transfers to third countries without adequacy determinations to assess whether the chosen transfer mechanism can effectively protect personal data in the destination country's legal environment, particularly assessing government access to data
keyActivities: Assess the destination country's data protection law, government access powers, and enforcement environment
Evaluate whether the chosen transfer mechanism (SCCs, BCRs) can be upheld in the destination country
Identify supplementary measures (encryption, pseudonymisation, contractual enhancements) where TIA reveals gaps
Document TIA findings, supplementary measures adopted, and residual risk acceptance
Review TIA when significant changes occur in the destination country's legal environment

maturity.basic: Basic research of destination country legal environment by DPO or legal; documented in transfer register; supplementary measures identified ad hoc
maturity.mature: Structured TIA template covering legal framework, government access, enforcement, and supplementary measures; DPO and legal sign-off on TIA; TIA stored in transfer register; review trigger for material legal changes in destination country
maturity.advanced: TIA integrated into cross-border transfer workflow with country risk intelligence feeds; automated alerts on significant legal changes in transfer destinations; TIA library covering common destination countries; third-party legal opinions obtained for high-risk transfers; TIA outcomes inform encryption and access control requirements

toolExamples: DLA Piper Global Data Protection Law
Custom TIA template with country research

aviation.additionalRequirements: PNR/API transfers to foreign authorities require documentation under s129; adequacy or derogation basis required

healthcare.additionalRequirements: Overseas telehealth providers must have s129-compliant transfer mechanisms

controls.name: Ongoing Cross-Border Transfer Monitoring
controls.description: Monitor existing cross-border transfer arrangements on an ongoing basis to ensure continued adequacy of transfer mechanisms, detect changes in the legal environment of destination countries, and identify new or unauthorized transfers requiring assessment and authorization
keyActivities: Review cross-border transfer register quarterly for accuracy and completeness
Monitor Commissioner announcements and regulatory updates affecting approved country list
Monitor legal developments in key transfer destination countries (legislation, court decisions)
Conduct annual re-assessment of transfer mechanisms for significant cross-border transfers
Implement process for employees to declare new cross-border data flows before implementation

maturity.basic: Annual review of transfer register by DPO; Commissioner website checked for approved country list updates; new vendors required to disclose international transfers to DPO
maturity.mature: Quarterly transfer register review with documented outcomes; regulatory monitoring service subscription covering key jurisdictions; annual mechanism re-assessment for top-10 cross-border transfers; IT procurement gate requires DPO approval for new international data flows
maturity.advanced: Real-time regulatory monitoring with automated alerts for transfer-relevant developments; CASB monitoring for unauthorized international data flows; automated annual re-assessment workflow; cross-border transfer risk score in third-party risk management dashboard; management reporting on cross-border transfer risk

aviation.additionalRequirements: PNR/API transfers to foreign authorities require documentation under s129; adequacy or derogation basis required

healthcare.additionalRequirements: Overseas telehealth providers must have s129-compliant transfer mechanisms

controls.name: Data Processor Due Diligence
controls.description: Conduct privacy and security due diligence on prospective data processors before engagement, assessing their technical and organizational measures to protect personal data and their ability to support the organization's PDPA compliance obligations
keyActivities: Classify all third parties by data access level and processing sensitivity
Send privacy due diligence questionnaire (covering security, privacy practices, subprocessors, cross-border transfers) to all significant processors
Review security certifications (ISO 27001, SOC 2 Type II, CSA STAR) as part of assessment
Assess processor's ability to support data subject rights requests and breach notification
Document assessment findings and risk rating before contract execution

maturity.basic: Due diligence questionnaire sent to processors handling significant personal data; certification copies reviewed; DPO approves engagement based on questionnaire responses
maturity.mature: Tiered due diligence programme based on processor risk rating; standardised questionnaire with scoring rubric; certification review against recognized standards; annual reassessment for high-risk processors; findings documented in third-party register
maturity.advanced: Automated due diligence platform with continuous processor risk scoring; integration with third-party risk intelligence services (BitSight, SecurityScorecard); onsite audit capability for critical processors; SLA for questionnaire completion; AI-assisted analysis of processor privacy policies and certifications

controls.name: Data Processing Agreement Lifecycle Management
controls.description: Ensure all data processors are subject to a binding data processing agreement (DPA) containing the mandatory provisions required to ensure the processor processes personal data only on the data controller's instructions and implements adequate security measures
keyActivities: Maintain a DPA template incorporating all minimum required provisions
Require DPA execution before any personal data is processed by a third-party processor
Review and update DPA template annually or following material regulatory changes
Track DPA execution status and renewal dates in the third-party processor register
Ensure DPAs with subprocessors of processors are in place and meet equivalent standards

maturity.basic: Standard DPA template reviewed by legal; DPAs executed for all identified processors; contracts stored in shared drive; manual tracking of DPA status
maturity.mature: DPA template library for different processing scenarios; automated contract expiry alerts; DPA clause library enabling negotiation while maintaining minimum standards; DPA status tracked in procurement system; annual DPA template review against regulatory guidance
maturity.advanced: AI-assisted contract review to identify DPA gaps in existing vendor agreements; automated DPA workflow integrated with vendor onboarding; contractual obligation tracking (breach notification SLAs, audit rights) in contract management platform; DPA compliance dashboard

controls.name: Ongoing Processor Oversight and Audit
controls.description: Maintain ongoing oversight of data processors through annual reassessments, review of updated certifications, and exercise of contractual audit rights where risk warrants, ensuring processors continue to meet the organization's data protection requirements
keyActivities: Conduct annual reassessment of high-risk processors using updated security questionnaire
Review processor security certifications for continued validity and scope coverage
Exercise contractual audit rights for critical processors handling large volumes of sensitive personal data
Review processor incident and breach reports and assess impact on the organization
Escalate processor compliance issues and document remediation requirements

maturity.basic: Annual email to key processors requesting updated security questionnaire; ISO 27001 certificate copies collected; issues escalated to DPO
maturity.mature: Tiered reassessment schedule (annual for high-risk, bi-annual for medium-risk); certification scope verification; contractual audit right exercised for top-5 critical processors annually; processor compliance dashboard in third-party register
maturity.advanced: Continuous security rating monitoring via BitSight/SecurityScorecard; automated reassessment workflow triggered by certification expiry or security rating decline; onsite or virtual audit program for critical processors; processor risk metrics integrated into organizational risk register; board reporting on third-party data risk

toolExamples: Contract management with audit right tracking

controls.name: Data Processor Offboarding and Data Return
controls.description: Implement a structured offboarding process for outgoing data processors ensuring all personal data held by the processor is returned or securely deleted, access credentials are revoked, and certificate of deletion is obtained in compliance with DPA terms and PDPA s10
keyActivities: Initiate offboarding checklist at contract termination or notice date
Request data return or deletion per DPA provisions (return first, then confirm deletion)
Obtain written certificate of data deletion from outgoing processor
Revoke all system access and API credentials on effective termination date
Archive DPA, due diligence records, and offboarding documentation in third-party register

maturity.basic: DPO emails outgoing processor to confirm data deletion; certificate received and filed; IT revokes access credentials
maturity.mature: Processor offboarding checklist with sign-off required before contract close; deletion certificate template in DPA; IT access revocation tracked; 30-day follow-up if certificate not received
maturity.advanced: Automated offboarding workflow integrated with contract management; technical verification of data deletion via API or audit log review where available; deletion certificate stored in third-party register; post-offboarding security scan for residual data; management reporting on offboarding completion rates

toolExamples: Contract lifecycle management with offboarding workflow

controls.name: Data Protection Officer Appointment and Functions
controls.description: Appoint a qualified Data Protection Officer (DPO) as required under PDPA s12A (inserted by Amendment Act A1727), register the DPO with the Commissioner, and ensure the DPO performs all required functions including advising on compliance, monitoring implementation, and acting as contact point for data subjects and the Commissioner
keyActivities: Assess mandatory DPO appointment obligation under s12A criteria and DPO Guideline 2025
Select and appoint DPO meeting minimum qualification requirements
Register appointed DPO with the Commissioner within prescribed period
Provide DPO with sufficient resources, authority, and independence to perform functions
Ensure DPO performs annual compliance monitoring and reports to senior management

maturity.basic: DPO appointed and registered; DPO performs compliance functions part-time alongside another role; annual report to management; Commissioner contact point established
maturity.mature: Dedicated DPO role with clear job description and reporting line to board; DPO with recognized certification (CIPM, CIPP); DPO participates in DPIA reviews, policy updates, incident response; DPO annual report with metrics presented to board
maturity.advanced: DPO function with supporting privacy team; DPO integrated into all major business decisions involving personal data; DPO maintains professional development plan; DPO participates in industry associations and regulatory engagement; DPO dashboard with real-time compliance metrics

toolExamples: GRC platforms for DPO function management

controls.name: Privacy Policy and Procedure Management
controls.description: Maintain a suite of up-to-date data protection policies and procedures covering all PDPA obligations, ensuring policies are approved at appropriate levels, communicated to staff, regularly reviewed, and aligned with the current regulatory environment including the 2024 Amendment Act A1727
keyActivities: Maintain a policy inventory covering all data protection obligations under PDPA
Conduct annual review of all policies against current PDPA requirements and Commissioner guidance
Obtain board or senior management approval for the overarching Data Protection Policy
Communicate policy updates to affected staff within a reasonable period of approval
Retire superseded policies and maintain archive of prior versions with effective dates

maturity.basic: Core data protection policy exists; updated when significant regulatory changes occur; stored on intranet; staff notified by email of major changes
maturity.mature: Complete policy suite covering all PDPA domains; annual policy review cycle with DPO sign-off; board approval for key policies; policy acknowledgement tracked in HR system; policy version control in document management system
maturity.advanced: Policy management platform with automated review reminders and approval workflows; regulatory change monitoring service triggers policy review; policy gap analysis against PDPA amendments; real-time policy compliance indicators; external counsel review of policy suite on significant regulatory change

controls.name: Staff Training and Awareness Programme
controls.description: Implement a structured data protection training and awareness programme ensuring all employees understand their PDPA obligations, can recognize personal data and sensitive personal data, know how to handle data subject requests, and can identify and report potential breaches
keyActivities: Deliver mandatory data protection induction training to all new employees within 30 days of start
Deliver annual refresher training to all staff covering key PDPA obligations and organizational policies
Develop role-specific training modules for DPO, IT security, HR, marketing, and customer service
Track training completions in training register and follow up on non-completions
Assess training effectiveness through knowledge assessments and breach/DSAR performance metrics

maturity.basic: Annual mandatory e-learning module on data protection basics; completion tracked by HR; new employee induction covers PDPA overview
maturity.mature: Role-differentiated training curriculum; compliance-themed scenarios in training content; assessment with minimum pass mark; non-completion escalated to line manager; training records integrated with HR system; phishing simulation to reinforce security awareness
maturity.advanced: Adaptive learning platform with AI-personalised training content by role and past assessment performance; micro-learning nudges on current breach scenarios; gamified compliance challenges; real-time training dashboard; training effectiveness measured against incident and DSAR performance trends; external certification support for privacy professionals

controls.name: Privacy by Design and Default
controls.description: Embed privacy considerations into the design and development of new products, services, and systems from inception, ensuring data protection requirements are addressed by design rather than as an afterthought, aligned with the accountability principles underlying PDPA compliance
keyActivities: Define privacy by design trigger criteria for new projects and material changes to existing systems
Conduct privacy design review using structured checklist at the design/architecture stage
Perform DPIA for high-risk processing activities identified at design stage
Obtain DPO sign-off as a mandatory gate before go-live for in-scope projects
Document privacy design decisions and accepted residual risks for each project

maturity.basic: DPO consulted on major new IT projects; privacy considerations checked against PDPA principles before launch; ad hoc documentation
maturity.mature: Privacy by design checklist integrated into project governance; DPIA trigger assessment mandatory for all new projects processing personal data; DPO review required for significant processing changes; privacy by design documented in project artefacts
maturity.advanced: Privacy engineering function embedded in product and IT teams; automated privacy gate in CI/CD pipeline; privacy design patterns library; DPIA integrated with project management tooling; privacy debt tracking for legacy systems; privacy metrics in product health dashboards

toolExamples: Threat modelling tools (Microsoft Threat Modeling Tool)

controls.name: Compliance Monitoring and Internal Audit
controls.description: Implement a compliance monitoring programme and annual internal audit of data protection practices to identify gaps against PDPA obligations, assess control effectiveness, and provide management with accurate information on the organization's compliance posture
keyActivities: Develop and execute an annual data protection audit programme based on risk priorities
Assess compliance against PDPA obligations using a structured compliance checklist
Review sample of consent records, DSARs, breach records, and training completions
Rate audit findings by severity and agree remediation timelines with management
Report audit findings and management responses to board or audit committee

maturity.basic: Annual self-assessment by DPO against PDPA checklist; findings reported to management; ad hoc remediation plans
maturity.mature: Annual compliance audit using structured methodology; internal audit or DPO-led; evidence-based assessment with sampling; findings rated by severity; remediation tracking to closure; board reporting
maturity.advanced: Risk-based audit programme with continuous monitoring between annual audits; automated compliance testing (consent record completeness, DSAR response rates, training completion); external verification for high-risk areas; audit outcomes feed into DPO annual report; benchmarking against industry peers and Commissioner guidance

controls.name: Board and Senior Management Reporting and Accountability
controls.description: Establish regular reporting of data protection compliance status, key risks, incidents, and emerging obligations to senior management and the board, ensuring leadership accountability for PDPA compliance and informed decision-making on data protection investment
keyActivities: Prepare and present DPO annual report to board covering compliance status, incidents, and recommendations
Provide quarterly management reporting on key data protection metrics (breach incidents, DSAR performance, training rates)
Ensure material data breaches are reported to board within 24 hours of notification decision
Include data protection risk in the organization's risk register with board visibility
Escalate regulatory changes and enforcement actions to board with assessment of impact

maturity.basic: Annual DPO report presented to management; significant incidents escalated ad hoc; data protection risk in IT risk register
maturity.mature: Quarterly management KPI dashboard for data protection; DPO annual report tabled at board; breach incidents reported to board within required timeframes; data protection risk in enterprise risk register with board visibility; management sign-off on DPO recommendations
maturity.advanced: Real-time executive data protection dashboard; monthly management briefing on emerging risks; board education sessions on PDPA and regulatory developments; data protection risk appetite statement approved by board; DPO participates in board audit/risk committee meetings; external benchmarking of compliance maturity presented to board

toolExamples: GRC platforms with board reporting module
PowerBI / Tableau for data protection dashboards

controls.name: Data Controller Registration Management
controls.description: Manage the organization's data controller registration with the Personal Data Protection Commissioner under PDPA s13-s16, including initial registration, timely renewal, notification of material changes, and DPO registration, to ensure the organization operates with valid registration at all times
keyActivities: Determine registration obligation and class under the Personal Data Protection (Class of Data Controllers) Order 2013
Apply for registration before commencing processing activities as a registered class data controller
Monitor registration expiry date and submit renewal application before expiry
Notify Commissioner of material changes to registered particulars within prescribed period
Register DPO with Commissioner as required under s12A

maturity.basic: DPO tracks registration renewal date manually; renewal submitted annually; certificate stored in compliance files; material changes notified when identified
maturity.mature: Registration calendar with automated renewal alerts at 90 and 30 days before expiry; registration details reviewed annually against ROPA for accuracy; material change notification checklist; DPO registration maintained current
maturity.advanced: Registration compliance integrated into GRC platform with automated tracking; change management process flags registration notification obligations; Commissioner portal monitored for regulatory updates to registration requirements; registration compliance included in annual audit scope

toolExamples: GRC platform for regulatory deadline tracking

controls.name: Regulatory Inspection Readiness
controls.description: Maintain the organization in a state of readiness for inspections, investigations, and audits by the JPDP Commissioner under PDPA Part VIII (s101-s109), including pre-organized compliance evidence, trained inspection response team, and documented response procedures
keyActivities: Maintain a compliance evidence repository with current copies of all key policies, registers, and records
Designate and train an inspection response team including DPO, legal counsel, and IT representative
Conduct annual mock inspection exercise to test readiness and identify gaps
Document inspection response procedures including immediate notification, document preservation, and liaison protocols
Review and update inspection readiness plan following each regulatory engagement or industry enforcement action

maturity.basic: DPO coordinates inspection response; key documents accessible; legal counsel on retainer; response guided by DPO judgment
maturity.mature: Written inspection response procedure; designated inspection response team with defined roles; compliance evidence repository maintained and indexed; annual readiness review; external legal counsel briefed on PDPA enforcement procedures
maturity.advanced: Formal inspection readiness programme with annual tabletop simulation; comprehensive compliance evidence repository with document index; privilege review procedure for legal communications; inspection response playbook covering all JPDP inspection powers; post-inspection review and learning embedded in governance programme

toolExamples: Document management system for evidence repository

controls.name: Enforcement Notice Compliance Management
controls.description: Manage compliance with enforcement notices issued by the Commissioner under PDPA Part IX, including legal assessment, compliance roadmap development, evidence collection, and Commissioner reporting, to avoid the criminal offence of non-compliance under PDPA s133
keyActivities: Immediately escalate enforcement notices to DPO, senior management, and legal counsel upon receipt
Obtain legal advice on notice requirements, compliance obligations, and appeal grounds
Develop a costed compliance roadmap with milestones within the notice compliance period
Maintain regular communication with Commissioner on compliance progress where appropriate
Compile and submit compliance evidence to Commissioner by the required deadline

maturity.basic: DPO and legal review enforcement notice; compliance actions assigned; evidence compiled and submitted to Commissioner by deadline
maturity.mature: Enforcement notice response procedure documented and tested; compliance roadmap template with milestone tracking; legal sign-off on Commissioner communications; post-compliance review to prevent recurrence
maturity.advanced: Enforcement notice risk assessment integrated with proactive compliance programme to prevent notices; rapid legal response protocol (within 24 hours); compliance roadmap managed via project management tool; Commissioner relationship management strategy; enforcement notice outcome used to update audit programme and training content

toolExamples: Project management tools (MS Project, Jira) for compliance roadmap

controls.name: Regulatory Monitoring and Change Management
controls.description: Systematically monitor PDPA regulatory developments including Commissioner guidelines, codes of practice, new subsidiary legislation, enforcement actions, and relevant court decisions, and assess their impact on the organization's compliance programme with timely implementation of required changes
keyActivities: Subscribe to and review JPDP Commissioner publications, press releases, and enforcement announcements
Monitor Parliament Hansard and Gazette for PDPA amendments and subsidiary legislation
Assess impact of each regulatory development on organizational policies, procedures, and controls
Implement required compliance changes within appropriate timeframes
Brief management and board on material regulatory developments and compliance implications

maturity.basic: DPO subscribes to JPDP email alerts; external legal counsel engaged for major regulatory changes; updates incorporated into policies annually
maturity.mature: Formal regulatory monitoring programme covering JPDP, Parliament, and comparable regional regulators; impact assessment for each significant development; change management procedure for implementing required updates; management briefings on significant changes
maturity.advanced: Regulatory technology platform monitoring Malaysian, ASEAN, and global privacy regulatory developments with AI-assisted impact analysis; regulatory change management integrated with policy management platform; regulatory intelligence briefings to board; engagement in Commissioner consultation processes; participation in industry associations for advance regulatory intelligence

toolExamples: Data Protection World Forum news alerts
